Description: The primary key uniquely identifies each transaction record.
The exact calendar date timestamp detailing when the transaction occurred.
Financial accounting period tracking monthly data continuity across fiscal years.
The unique identifier assigned to the specific family unit or household.
The broader geographic region where the household resides.
The specific city, municipality, or province of the household.
The total count of individuals living within the household.

Constraints: Must be greater than or equal to 0
Description: The total number of individuals in the household financially supported by the primary earners.
The total take-home income of the household for the month, after taxes and deductions.
The economic bracket of the family.
The legal or ownership status of the family's primary residence.
A summary of the family's primary modes of transportation and commuting habits.
A summary of the household dependents schooling status.
The classification of the financial event.
Indicates whether the transaction represents money coming into the household or leaving it.
The expense classification grouping transactions into fund allocation.

Allowable Values: Internet', 'Entertainment', 'Streaming', 'Drinking Water', 'School Allowance', 'Rent', 'Fast Food', 'Loan Payment', 'School Snacks', 'Tricycle Fare', 'Unknown', 'School Supplies', 'Bus/UV Fare', 'Side Hustle', 'Groceries', 'Public Market', 'Personal Care', 'Savings Deposit', 'School Project', 'Dental', 'Emergency Treatment', 'Salary', 'Rice', 'Checkup', 'Water Bill', 'Gifts', 'Appliance Repair', 'LPG', 'Emergency Fund Contribution', 'Jeepney Fare', 'PTA/School Fee', 'Electricity', 'Clothing', 'Medicine', 'Sari-sari Store'
Description: The granular classification that breaks down each category into specific spending types.
The name of the business, vendor, or the service provider, identifying where or to whom the payment was made.
A short narrative providing context or reason for the transaction.
The monetary value of the transaction in Philippine peso, including negative values for refunded transactions.
The channel is utilized to settle transactions or the financial expense.
The medium through which the transaction took place.
The behavioral classification that tags each transaction based on its financial priority.
Indicates if the transaction is a repeating event.
The specific budget bucket to which this transaction is applied.
The total amount of money allocated to the budget category for the current month
The cadence at which the budget allocation is distributed or reviewed.
The specific family member role responsible for or associated with the transaction.
A binary indicator of whether the expense is specifically for educational purposes.
A binary indicator of whether the expense is for medical or healthcare needs.
A binary indicator of whether the transaction relates to loan repayment or debt servicing.
A binary indicator of whether the transaction involves placing money into savings.
The current processing state of the transaction.
Evidence flag confirming if a physical or digital receipt exists for the record.
An optional free-text field for supplementary remarks about the transaction.
